title: contrast-ratio rule ignores surfaceColor/onSurfaceColor token pairs
body: contrastCheck() in contrast-ratio.ts only looks at `backgroundColor`/`textColor` properties on a component. Our DESIGN.md uses `surfaceColor`/`onSurfaceColor` naming for card-like components and the linter never flags genuinely low-contrast pairs there. Could the rule check a configurable list of color-pair property names?

title: Add SCSS variables as an export format
body: `design.md export` currently supports css-tailwind, json-tailwind, dtcg, and css-vars per the FORMATS list in export.ts. Our design system pipeline uses SCSS variables rather than CSS custom properties — would a `scss` format emitting `$name: value;` be in scope?

title: missing-primary rule doesn't recognize 'brand' as the base color
body: The missing-primary lint rule flags our DESIGN.md as missing a primary color token, but we intentionally name it `brand` instead of `primary` throughout the file (and reference it consistently). Could the rule accept an alias list, or at least document that `primary` is a hardcoded required name?

title: Unclear precedence between DESIGN.md tokens and an existing tailwind.config.js
body: In the atmospheric-glass example, tailwind.config.js already defines colors, and `design.md export --format tailwind` also emits color tokens. Which one wins when both define the same key isn't documented anywhere — do I need to remove the config values or does the exported file always override?

title: orphaned-tokens rule test fixtures don't cover nested component references
body: Looking at orphaned-tokens.test.ts, all fixtures reference tokens directly from top-level components. We hit a case where a token was only referenced from a nested/composed component definition and the rule (incorrectly, we think) flagged it as orphaned. Could a nested-reference fixture be added to pin down expected behavior?
